content: Routing hardening follow-up after v0.1.1: reduced unnecessary doglegs by treating same X/Y as valid unless it creates exact endpoint overlap, insufficient spacing, component intersection, or long segment overlap. Endpoint deconfliction is scoped by node side, direct/low-bend routes are preferred before axis-conflict avoidance, and final straightening now also handles mixed-side endpoint-preserving L routes. Added a local-first UI fixture from the browser report and fixed inward endpoint routes by reselecting source/target ports only when the first or last segment would turn back through its own component. The ArchMap core architecture fixture still keeps Layout->Renderer to three points, and the local-first fixture now asserts rectangle endpoints move outward from their component faces. Verification: npm test -- --run test/layout.test.ts, npm test -- --run test/render.test.ts, npm test -- --run test/edges.test.ts test/layout.test.ts test/render.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build. Existing untracked reports/ left untouched.
